narrative:
Karnataka High Court heard **1,082 of 3,089 listed matters**, an overall hearing rate of **35.0%**. That is lower than the available recent benchmark of **42.0% on 2026-04-25** and **43.2% on 2026-04-23**, but above **31.5% on 2026-04-24**. The day’s throughput was therefore mid-range relative to recent sittings: a larger absolute volume was heard than on those earlier days, but against a much heavier cause list.

Judge-wise, the strongest conversion on meaningful dockets came from **K. Manmadha Rao (51 of 63, 81.0%)**, **V. Srishananda (66 of 98, 67.3%)**, **E.S. Indiresh (82 of 122, 67.2%)**, and **S.R. Krishna Kumar (91 of 156, 58.3%)**. **M. Nagaprasanna** carried the heaviest combined roster at **418 scheduled** and still heard **125 matters**, the highest absolute disposal count in the dataset, though at a modest **29.9%** conversion. The weakest large-list outcomes were **S.G. Pandit & K. V. Aravind (5 of 365, 1.4%)**, **Sachin Shankar Magadum (23 of 205, 11.2%)**, and **S. Rachaiah (22 of 176, 12.5%)**.

Stage conversion was led by **Admission (45.3%)** and **Preliminary Hearing (40.3%)**, while **Orders**, despite being the largest bucket at **1,164 scheduled**, converted only **28.4%**. That is a sharp drop from **46.3% on 2026-04-25** and **48.5% on 2026-04-23**, making Orders the main drag on today’s overall rate. By case type, **WP** dominated volume with **1,319 scheduled and 510 heard**, contributing nearly half of all heard matters, with a **38.7%** conversion. **MFA (53.0%)** and **CRL.RP (68.5%)** showed strong clearance, while **WA** remained a major bottleneck at just **25 of 418 heard (6.0%)**, broadly in line with the weak **5.2%** seen on 2026-04-24.
